title: Ctenopteris rigens
taxonomy: Ctenopteris rigens is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenopteris, which is a group of ferns that are native to Australia and New Zealand. The classification of Ctenopteris rigens is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification indicates that Ctenopteris rigens is a type of vascular plant that reproduces via spores. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are known for their leathery fronds and are commonly found in forests and woodlands.
uses: Ctenopteris rigens, also known as the New Zealand tree fern, has been used by the indigenous Maori people for various medicinal purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Traditionally, the Maori would use the plant to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Further studies are required to fully validate the traditional uses of Ctenopteris rigens.

intro: Ctenopteris rigens, also known as the New Zealand fern or Cabbage Tree Fern, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. The health benefits of Ctenopteris rigens include its potential in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant.
